tool.name: skipseq
tool.summary: Skip the first N sequence records and return the remainder

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `skipseq` removes a non-negative number of leading sequence records from a local input stream and returns the remaining records in stable source order. The tool reuses the shared Epithema sequence loader and sequence-collection result path rather than embedding format-specific stream handling.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts one local sequence input path plus one non-negative skip count. The count is interpreted as the number of leading records to discard before emitting the remainder.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits the remaining sequence records through the shared FASTA output path. CLI output also includes the standard Epithema method summary lines reporting the input path, effective skipped count, total input count, returned record count, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema skipseq`. Validation currently covers zero-skip behavior, single-record skipping, interior skip counts, skip-all, skip-beyond-end, and malformed or empty-input failure behavior.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports one input path and one skip count per invocation. Negative skip counts are rejected by argument parsing. Skip counts greater than the number of records are clamped to the input length and return an empty output stream rather than failing.

artifacts.label: Three-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed multi-record FASTA fixture used to validate stable skip behavior.

examples.title: Skip the first record from a three-record FASTA input
examples.description: Drops the first record from a small FASTA fixture and returns the remaining suffix in original order.

expected_outputs.label: Remaining sequence collection
expected_outputs.description: A FASTA sequence collection containing the second and third records from the source fixture.
